heading.title: Apps
heading.subtitle: Turn a data source Tool into an interactive interface for compatible AI hosts.

placeholder.name: Orders table
placeholder.description: Explain what this App helps the user do

empty.noApps: No Apps yet. Create an App from one of your data source Tools.
empty.noSources: Create a data source with at least one enabled Tool before creating an App.
empty.noTools: This data source has no enabled Tools.

view.tableHint: Best for lists with consistent fields.

create.title: Create App
create.sourceHint: The App reuses its Tool, authentication, limits, and execution settings.
create.toolHint: One App can be attached to each enabled Tool.
create.viewHint: Choose how structuredContent should be presented by compatible AI hosts.
create.reviewHint: The App will be published with the selected Tool on this data source's MCP endpoint.

detail.title: App settings
detail.publicationHint: Connect an MCP-compatible AI host to the URL below. When it calls this Tool, the host can open the App resource URI as an interactive interface.

help.title: How MCP Apps work
help.intro: An MCP App adds a visual interface to an existing Tool. The model still chooses and calls the Tool; a compatible host then renders the returned structured data in the App.
help.source: The linked data source remains responsible for API access, authentication, secrets, limits, and Tool execution.
help.view: Arthur publishes the App as an MCP UI resource and associates it with the Tool. Table, cards, details, and JSON views do not execute arbitrary custom code.
help.host: Use the data source's MCP connection URL in a host that supports MCP Apps. Hosts without Apps support can still consume the Tool's text fallback.

confirm.deleteMessage: The Tool remains available, but its interactive App will no longer be published.

error.forbidden: You do not have permission to access Apps.
